title: carex retrorsa var. bradleyi
intro: Carex retrorsa var. bradleyi, also known as Bradley's sedge, is a variety of flowering plant in the genus Carex. It is a type of sedge that is native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada, and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, Bradley's sedge is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be used to make a topical salve or ointment for skin irritations.
